title: God mode collapse to mode-config (eliminate parallel agent fork)
current_state: agent/god.py is 633 lines of parallel implementation: own tool dispatch (_TOOLS registry, 20 hand-written tools at god.py:561-582), own memory layer (GodMemory class, separate SQLite schema at god.py:63-257), own ticket system (GodTickets at god.py:264-346), own LLM call layer (_groq_call, _ollama_call, custom _llm at god.py:396-433, bypasses LLMRouter entirely), own tool-call format (regex-parsed ```tool``` JSON fences at god.py:42, 614-633, not Anthropic tool-use blocks), no evolution tracking (agent.evolution.track_pattern never invoked in god mode).
structural_problem: Every improvement to the main agent silently does not apply to god mode. Drift is guaranteed by construction. Already happening: god mode cannot use Cerebras (S-053), does not benefit from T-078 temporal validity windows (GodMemory has no invalid_at column), cannot use the 53 newer non-god tools. When user invokes god mode they assume Pi-capabilities transfer; they silently do not.
target_state: God mode is a ModeConfig instance in agent/modes.py with: prompt_path=prompts/god_consciousness.txt, memory_db=data/god_memory.db, memory_namespace='god', vault_path=vault/.god/, tickets_dir=tickets/god/, router_tier='private' (routes Ollama first when set). The main response method handles god mode through the unified code path. All 73 tools work. All memory pipeline improvements (incl. S-054) apply.
files_affected: agent/god.py (delete after migration; archive to docs/_archive/agent_god_v1.py)
pi_agent.py (remove _respond_god method; route god through unified _respond)
core/llm_router.py (add private=True flag; provider preference Ollama-first when set)
tools/tools_memory.py (accept db_path and namespace params; ensure invalid_at column applies to god_memory.db)
tickets/god/ (unchanged — existing ticket tools point here when in god mode)
vault/.god/ (unchanged — vault sync reads from ModeConfig.vault_path)
success_criteria: agent/god.py no longer importable from pi_agent.py; archived to docs/_archive/
wc -l pi_agent.py drops by at least 50 lines (the _respond_god path)
God mode reachable via 'god mode' command; interactive smoke test passes
New test tests/test_god_uses_unified_path.py asserts god mode flows through LLMRouter and agent/tools.py::execute_tool
Test asserts S-054 temporal validity (invalid_at) works in god_memory.db (same MemoryTools)
risk_notes: Touching the privacy boundary. Mitigation: feature-flag the migration. Keep agent/god.py callable until new path passes all tests, then archive. Privacy guarantee remains: data/god_memory.db, prompts/god_consciousness.txt, tickets/god/, vault/.god/ all gitignored. Privacy is .gitignore + config, never code duplication (P7).
migration_plan: 1. Write docs/adr/001-god-as-mode-config.md justifying the collapse and listing the privacy invariants preserved
2. Add ModeConfig dataclass to agent/modes.py with fields: name, prompt_path, memory_db, memory_namespace, vault_path, tickets_dir, router_tier, supports_tools, max_tokens
3. Register three configs: root, normie, god
4. Extend MemoryTools to accept db_path and namespace; ensure all memory operations (incl. invalidate) work against god_memory.db
5. Extend LLMRouter to accept tier='private' that prefers Ollama over cloud providers
6. Build new unified _respond path in pi_agent.py that consumes ModeConfig (initially shadow-mode, called only by tests)
7. Switch god mode dispatch from _respond_god to unified _respond; keep _respond_god in place as fallback
8. Run full god-mode smoke tests; verify all 20 old god tools work via new dispatch
9. Remove _respond_god from pi_agent.py; archive agent/god.py to docs/_archive/agent_god_v1.py
10. Append solutions/SOLUTIONS.jsonl entry with root_cause, files_changed, tests, better_future_fix (e.g. private memory deserves its own L1/L2 distillation policy), not_done
